title: Melica canescens
intro: Melica canescens, also known as the gray melic grass, is a perennial grass species native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, stress, and digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of Melica canescens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Melica canescens include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote relaxation and support overall well-being.
taxonomy: Melica canescens is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is a type of grass that belongs to the genus Melica. The classification of Melica canescens is further broken down as follows: it is a member of the tribe Meliceae, subfamily Pooideae, and order Poales. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and morphological features. The exact classification of Melica canescens is Melica canescens (Trin.) Kunth, a synonym for Melica ciliata subsp. ciliata.

The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions.
